source_name: FAQ - RETURN OF ALLOTMENT OF SHARES (ROA) AND REGISTER OF MEMBERS (ROM)
part: REGISTER OF MEMBERS (ROM)
section: REGISTER OF MEMBERS (ROM)
section_title: Do all Return Of Allotment (ROA) or formerly known as Form 24 must be lodged online through MyCoID System?
content:
Q: Do all Return Of Allotment (ROA) or formerly known as Form 24 must be lodged online through MyCoID System?

A: Yes, all Return of Allotment of Shares (ROA) occurring after 31 January 2017 must be lodged online (MyCoID 2016).

source_name: FAQ - RETURN OF ALLOTMENT OF SHARES (ROA) AND REGISTER OF MEMBERS (ROM)
part: REGISTER OF MEMBERS (ROM)
section: REGISTER OF MEMBERS (ROM)
section_title: If the company has a period for the date of allotment, what is the date used for the calculation of late lodgement?
content:
Q: If the company has a period for the date of allotment, what is the date used for the calculation of late lodgement?

A: If the company has a period for the date of allotment, the date of lodgement of the calculation are calculated the earliest date of the allotment.

source_name: FAQ - RETURN OF ALLOTMENT OF SHARES (ROA) AND REGISTER OF MEMBERS (ROM)
part: REGISTER OF MEMBERS (ROM)
section: REGISTER OF MEMBERS (ROM)
section_title: If I had lodged the ROA, do I need to lodge the ROM?
content:
Q: If I had lodged the ROA, do I need to lodge the ROM?

A: Yes. The ROA is simply to update the information on the allotment of shares. Whereas the ROM is for the updates on the members information.

source_name: FAQ - RETURN OF ALLOTMENT OF SHARES (ROA) AND REGISTER OF MEMBERS (ROM)
part: REGISTER OF MEMBERS (ROM)
section: REGISTER OF MEMBERS (ROM)
section_title: Can I lodge the ROA and the ROM over the counter because I have not registered as a user registration?
content:
Q: Can I lodge the ROA and the ROM over the counter because I have not registered as a user registration?

A: No. Please register as a user registration beforehand at any nearest SSM office.

section_title: What is the fee charged for the lodgement of ROA and ROM?
content:
Q: What is the fee charged for the lodgement of ROA and ROM?

A: Currently, there is no fee until notified further by SSM.

section_title: Do I need to update the list of members through the ROM in which it occurred before 31 January 2017? Does the payment fo
content:
Q: Do I need to update the list of members through the ROM in which it occurred before 31 January 2017? Does the payment for late lodgement rates apply?

A: Yes. Late lodgement payment is only imposed for event dates which occurred after 31 January 2017.

section_title: When do I need to lodge the ROM if there is a change in the register of members?
content:
Q: When do I need to lodge the ROM if there is a change in the register of members?

A: The ROM needs to be lodged within 14 days from the date of the change (Refer section 51(1) of the Companies Act 2016)

section_title: Is the Extension of Time (EOT) applicable for the ROA and the ROM?
content:
Q: Is the Extension of Time (EOT) applicable for the ROA and the ROM?

A: Yes. The company may apply for an EOT under section 609(2) of the Companies Act

section_title: Can the company secretary who is blacklisted lodge the ROM and ROA?
content:
Q: Can the company secretary who is blacklisted lodge the ROM and ROA?

A: No. Please ensure that you are not blacklisted by referring to the Compliance Division.

section_title: If I have a problem during or after the lodgement of ROA and ROM is made, how do I overcome this?
content:
Q: If I have a problem during or after the lodgement of ROA and ROM is made, how do I overcome this?

A: Please email to enquiry@ssm.com.my or contact the contact center line 03-7721 4000.

section_title: If the allotment is non-cash (otherwise) should the supporting document be provided?
content:
Q: If the allotment is non-cash (otherwise) should the supporting document be provided?

A: Yes. The company still needs to provide the supporting document and uploaded in the MyCoID in pdf and tiff format.

section_title: If the allotment of shares or the changes to the information of members occurred before 31 January 2017, can I send it t
content:
Q: If the allotment of shares or the changes to the information of members occurred before 31 January 2017, can I send it through online?

A: No. A document dated before 31 January 2017 should be lodged in the format of Form 24 as required under the Companies Act 1965.

section_title: Can I park two (2) prices on the 'price per share' for a same type of share in one ROA? If not, why?
content:
Q: Can I park two (2) prices on the 'price per share' for a same type of share in one ROA? If not, why?

A: No. This is because the 'price per share' refers to the market value.

section_title: What if in the ROA the 'price per share; refers to a different type of share on the same date? Should the price per shar
content:
Q: What if in the ROA the 'price per share; refers to a different type of share on the same date? Should the price per share be the same or otherwise?

A: If the 'price per share' refers to a different type of share, a different 'price per share' is allowed to be used.

section_title: In the event a company is required to provide confirmation of its latest shareholding structure or details of its shareh
content:
Q: In the event a company is required to provide confirmation of its latest shareholding structure or details of its shareholders in the company to third parties, what documents can the company provide to support the information to be made available, especially if the information is requested by Ministries, Government agencies or for Government procurement purposes? (updated on 31 December 2024)

A: Pursuant to Section 50(1) of the Companies Act 2016 ("the Act"), every company is required to maintain a register of its members (register of members) and keep it at the company's registered office or another place in accordance with Section 47 of the Act. While Section 50(3) of the Act stipulates that the register of members constitutes prima facie evidence of any matters inserted in the register as required or authorized by the Act. Further, Section 51(1) of the Act requires companies to promptly notify the Registrar of any changes in the particulars in their register of members. The notification to the Registrar must be made within 14 days from the date of the changes. Consequently, the latest shareholding structure of a company can be obtained from SSM, subject to payment of the prescribed fee stipulated under Regulation 8 of the Companies Regulation 2017. However, pursuant to Section 51(3) of the Act, companies whose shares are quoted on a stock exchange are not required to notify the Registrar of any changes in their register of members. Due to this exception and the 14-day timeframe for the notification under Section 51(1) of the Act, the Registrar advises that the most latest particulars regarding a company's shareholding structure or composition can be sourced directly from the company secretary. On this note, Ministries, Government agencies or any third-party requiring evidence or confirmation of the latest shareholding structure or composition of a company may, in addition to the company information printout from SSM, rely on the
